prompts.name: telemetry-best-practices
prompts.description: Follow these best practices for the RDK telemetry project
prompts.prompt:
You are working on an RDK telemetry service that handles data collection and reporting. Follow these guidelines:
- Use proper error handling and logging
- Follow memory management best practices
- Ensure thread safety in concurrent operations
- Validate all input parameters
- Follow RDK coding standards
- Use appropriate data structures for performance

prompts.name: security-requirements
prompts.prompt:
Ensure all code follows these security practices:
- Validate buffer sizes to prevent overflows
- Sanitize all input data
- Use secure memory handling functions
- Protect sensitive data
- Implement proper access controls
- Follow secure coding guidelines

prompts.name: data-handling
prompts.prompt:
When handling telemetry data:
- Validate data format and structure
- Implement proper data serialization
- Handle data privacy requirements
- Implement efficient data processing
- Follow data retention policies
- Ensure proper error handling for data operations

prompts.name: bus-interface
prompts.prompt:
For CCSP and RBUS interface implementations:
- Ensure proper message handling
- Implement retry mechanisms
- Handle connection failures gracefully
- Follow bus protocol specifications
- Implement proper error reporting

prompts.name: reporting
prompts.prompt:
For report generation code:
- Implement efficient data aggregation
- Handle large datasets appropriately
- Follow report format specifications
- Implement proper error handling
- Ensure accurate data representation

prompts.name: performance-optimization
prompts.prompt:
When optimizing performance:
- Identify and eliminate bottlenecks
- Use efficient algorithms and data structures
- Minimize memory allocations and copies
- Use profiling tools to guide optimizations
- Follow best practices for concurrency and parallelism

prompts.name: runs-on-low-end-embedded-devices
prompts.description: Guidelines for code that runs on low-end embedded devices
prompts.prompt:
When writing code for low-end embedded devices:
- Optimize for low memory usage
- Minimize CPU cycles
- Use fixed-point arithmetic where applicable
- Avoid dynamic memory allocation
- Ensure compatibility with limited hardware resources
- Implement power-efficient algorithms
